tg.cmd.bridge: Link active Cursor tabs to forum threads
tg.cmd.bridge_all: Topics for all tabs and windows
tg.cmd.setup_tg_send: Enable photo/file relay in this project
tg.fmt.questionnaireFreeformHint: For “Other”: tap the button, then reply to the bot message with your answer.
tg.fmt.notifyMode.quiet: quiet — no activity/thoughts; tools when done; no assistant streaming while busy
tg.fmt.notifyMode.final: final — silent while agent busy; final assistant reply only
tg.msg.register.usage:
Usage: /register <token>

Token is in the server log (Output / terminal).
tg.msg.register.notAllowed: Access restricted — add your Telegram user ID in Handoff settings (step 2) or Cursor Settings.
tg.msg.register.ok: Registered {name}! All bot commands are available.
tg.msg.bridge.notSupergroup:
⚠️ This is not a supergroup.

1. Group settings
2. Enable Topics (group becomes supergroup)
3. Run /bridge again
tg.msg.bridge.topicsOff:
⚠️ Forum topics are not enabled.

1. Group settings > Topics > Enable
2. Run /bridge again

The bot cannot enable topics — only the group owner can.
tg.msg.bridge.notAdmin:
⚠️ Bot is not an administrator.

Group settings > Administrators > add the bot with:
• Manage topics
• Delete messages
• Pin messages

Then run /bridge again.
tg.msg.bridge.adminCheckFailed: ⚠️ Could not verify bot permissions (Telegram API error). Retry /bridge shortly.
tg.msg.bridge.enabledNoCursor: ✅ Bridge enabled. Cursor not connected yet — threads will be created automatically when it connects.
tg.msg.bridge.enabledNoTabs:
✅ Bridge enabled. No active tabs with messages yet — threads appear when chats start.
({windows} window(s), {withTabs} with tabs)
tg.msg.bridge.enabledAllExist: ✅ Bridge enabled. All threads already exist.
tg.msg.bridge.enabledCreating: ✅ Bridge enabled. Creating {count} thread(s) in the background. Bot stays responsive.
tg.msg.bridge.notEnabled: ⚠️ Bridge is not enabled. Run /bridge first.
tg.msg.bridge_all.noTabs: No active tabs with messages. Start a chat in Cursor and retry.
tg.msg.bridge_all.creating: Creating threads for {count} tabs in the background…
tg.msg.project.windowTimeout: ⚠️ Timed out waiting for project window {name}.
tg.msg.project.switchFailed: ⚠️ Window opened but could not switch: {error}
tg.msg.project.threadFailed: ⚠️ Chat created but Telegram thread failed: {error}
tg.msg.openProject.notFound:
⚠️ Nothing found for «{query}».
Searching standard folders only: Projects/dev/code/src.
tg.msg.openProject.pick: Found several projects for «{query}». Pick a button:
tg.msg.projects.none: ⚠️ No projects found in standard folders.
tg.msg.webUrl.restarting: ⚠️ Domain unavailable — restarting cloudflared, will send a new link…
tg.msg.webUrl.failed: ❌ Could not start tunnel. Check VPN, cloudflared, and cloudflared-quick.log in the Handoff runtime data folder (Handoff settings).
tg.msg.unbridge.none: Bridge disabled. No tracked threads to delete.
tg.msg.closeChat.threadOnly: ⚠️ /close_chat — only inside a project Telegram thread, not in # General.
tg.msg.threadNotMapped: ⚠️ This thread is not linked. Run /bridge first.
tg.msg.closeChat.ok: ✅ Chat «{title}» closed in Cursor. This Telegram thread is still linked to it. Delete the thread manually if not needed. New chat — /new_chat (creates a separate thread).
tg.msg.newChat.threadOnly: ⚠️ /new_chat — only inside a project Telegram thread, not in # General.
tg.msg.newChat.inFlight: ⏳ /new_chat already running — one command = one tab, please wait.
tg.msg.newChat.bridgeOff: ⚠️ Bridge disabled. Run /bridge first — new threads won't be created without it.
tg.msg.newChat.threadFailed: ⚠️ Chat created in Cursor but Telegram thread failed: {error}
tg.msg.newChat.ok: ✅ New chat: {name} Write in the new thread.
tg.msg.merge_threads.none: ✅ No duplicate threads. Each (project, tab) → one Telegram thread.
tg.msg.merge_threads.running: 🧹 Merging threads: deleting {drop} extra thread(s) in {groups} groups…
tg.msg.merge_threads.donePartial: ✅ Merged threads: deleted {deleted} ({failed} failed — bindings removed).
tg.msg.flush.running: 🗑 Deleting all threads in the background…
tg.msg.flush.done: ✅ Purge complete: deleted {deleted} thread(s). Run /bridge to set up.
tg.msg.flush.empty: ✅ No threads found — group is clean. Run /bridge to set up.
tg.msg.status.title: Status
tg.msg.threadStatus.generalOnly: ⚠️ /thread_status — only in a project thread, not in # General.
tg.msg.lastCommit.generalOnly: ⚠️ /last_commit — only in a project thread, not in # General.
tg.msg.lastCommit.noWorkspace: ⚠️ Workspace path unknown for this thread.
tg.msg.lastCommit.empty: ⚠️ No commits in this repository yet.
tg.msg.whereami.title: Where traffic goes
tg.msg.whereami.footer: Inbound from this thread → switch to binding → send to Cursor.
tg.msg.whereami.generalOnly: ⚠️ /whereami — only in a project thread, not in # General.
tg.msg.notifyMode.generalOnly: ⚠️ /notify_mode — only in a project thread, not in # General.
tg.msg.notifyMode.usage: Usage: /notify_mode full|quiet|final Current: {current} full — all updates (activity, tools, streaming). quiet — no activity/thoughts/queue; tools when done; no assistant edits while busy. final — silent while agent busy; then final assistant reply only (no tools/diffs). Approve, questionnaire, Run/Skip, plan — always, all modes.
tg.msg.mode.loadFailed: Current mode: {mode} Could not load mode list from Cursor.
tg.msg.model.autoOn: Auto is enabled.{desc} Run /auto_off first, then /pick_model again.
tg.msg.model.autoOff.ok: ✅ Auto is off. Run /pick_model to choose a model.
tg.msg.model.autoOff.fail: ⚠️ Could not turn off Auto in Cursor. Open the composer and try again.
tg.msg.model.autoOff.stillOn: ⚠️ Auto is still on in Cursor. Try again or toggle it in the IDE.
tg.msg.model.enableAuto.ok: ✅ Auto is on. Cursor picks the model for you.
tg.msg.model.enableAuto.fail: ⚠️ Could not turn on Auto in Cursor. Open the composer and try again.
tg.msg.model.enableAuto.stillOff: ⚠️ Auto is still off in Cursor. Try again or toggle it in the IDE.
tg.msg.model.loadFailed: Current model: {model} Could not load model list from Cursor.
tg.msg.callback.pickWrongChat: This pick is for another chat.
tg.msg.callback.actionStale: Action no longer pending (done, cleared, or restart).
tg.msg.callback.extractFailed: Could not extract content — tool call may have left the DOM.
tg.msg.callback.planNotFound: Plan not found in current state
tg.msg.callback.freeformHint: ✏️ Reply to this message with your answer — it goes into the survey field, not the main prompt.
tg.msg.pause.ok: ⏸ CursorWake paused. /resume or tray checkbox to continue.
tg.msg.general.notConnected: ⚠️ CursorHandoff is not connected to Cursor IDE. Open Cursor with --remote-debugging-port=9222 and wait for «Connected again». Agent messages — only in a project thread, not in # General.
tg.msg.general.commandsOnly: ℹ️ # General — commands only (/status etc.). Write agent tasks in a project thread.
tg.msg.cursorUpgrade: Cursor {cursorVersion} — this Handoff build targets Cursor {testedCursorVersion}. Full functionality is not guaranteed. Rebuild or install the current VSIX.
tg.msg.setupTg.generalOnly: ⚠️ Send photos in a project thread, not General.
tg.msg.setupTg.notMappedBridge: ⚠️ Thread not linked. Run /bridge or write from an active Cursor tab.
tg.msg.setupTg.noProjectPath:
⚠️ No project path for this thread.
Open the Cursor window for this project and try again.
tg.msg.setupTg.already:
✅ Photo/file send to Telegram is already set up in this project.

Ask the agent freely — screenshot, photo, multiple files.
It puts results in outbox; I deliver here.
tg.msg.setupTg.writeFailed:
⚠️ Could not create folders and rules in the project (no write access).
Check directory permissions and retry.
tg.msg.setupTg.done:
✅ Done — photo/file send to Telegram is set up in this project.

Ask the agent: «take a screenshot … and send here».
Output goes to outbox; I deliver here (multiple files OK).
tg.msg.photo.expired: ⏱ Time expired — photo cancelled. Send again: photo with caption or photo then text.
tg.msg.questionnaireFreeform.expired: ⏱ Time to answer “Other” expired — tap Other again, then reply to the bot message.
tg.msg.questionnaireFreeform.replyToHint: ↩️ Use Reply on the bot message above — your text goes into the survey, not the main prompt.
tg.msg.questionnaireFreeform.noTextarea: Other textarea not found — tap Other again.
tg.msg.photo.awaitingText:
📎 Got file. Waiting for text (up to 10 min) — caption or next message.
Then I'll send everything to Cursor.
tg.msg.inbound.unsupportedType:
⚠️ This message type is not supported: {type}.
Send a file, photo, video, voice, GIF, or text with a caption.
tg.msg.force.empty: ⚠️ Empty message after $. Write text after $.
tg.msg.inbound.notConnected: ⚠️ Not connected to Cursor IDE. Wait for reconnect or restart the server in Cursor.
tg.msg.queue.fileForwardFailed: ⚠️ File forward: could not send file: {error}
tg.msg.window.switchFailed: ⚠️ Could not switch to target window.
tg.msg.window.openHint: Tip: open the project in Cursor and run /bridge here to save the folder path.
ext.handoffSettings.title: Handoff settings
ext.handoffSettings.subtitle: Bind address, web password, Telegram bridge, Wake tray, and optional Cloudflare tunnel.
ext.handoffSettings.nav.label: Sections
ext.handoffSettings.hero.accessSub: Who can reach your server on port {port}
ext.handoffSettings.hero.extrasSub: Queue and tunnel when Cursor is off
ext.handoffSettings.nexus.graphHint: Tap a node to open its module
ext.handoffSettings.ui.setupFlow: Complete these steps in order — each unlocks the next.
ext.handoffSettings.addons.lead: Optional extras — install after Web access and Telegram are set up.
ext.handoffSettings.hub.tagline: Phone bridge for your local Cursor session.
ext.handoffSettings.language.label: Language
ext.handoffSettings.language.hint: Applies to Handoff settings, the sidebar, status bar, phone web UI, and Telegram replies.
ext.handoffSettings.network.lead: Read and write in Cursor from a phone browser while Handoff runs on this PC.
ext.handoffSettings.network.s1: Handoff serves a web UI on this PC while Cursor is running.
ext.handoffSettings.network.s2: Open the link on your phone — same chats as on the desktop.
ext.handoffSettings.network.s3: LAN and Custom reach beyond this machine — set a password first.
ext.handoffSettings.network.endpointHint: Your link — copy after saving bind settings below.
ext.handoffSettings.network.bindIntro: Localhost — this PC only. LAN — Wi‑Fi devices. Custom — Tailscale or a fixed IP.
ext.handoffSettings.network.title: Bind
ext.handoffSettings.network.lan.title: LAN
ext.handoffSettings.network.lan.hint: 0.0.0.0 — devices on your Wi‑Fi. Set a password first.
ext.handoffSettings.network.custom.title: Custom
ext.handoffSettings.network.custom.hint: Tailscale or another interface IP to bind to.
ext.handoffSettings.network.passwordFirs: Set a password before LAN, Tailscale, or Cloudflare.
ext.handoffSettings.network.tailscaleHin: On Tailscale? Step-by-step in the guide.
ext.handoffSettings.password.title: Password
ext.handoffSettings.password.description: Asked when opening the link from another device.
ext.handoffSettings.password.browserHint: Open in a browser on another device:
ext.handoffSettings.telegram.lead: Five steps, top to bottom. Each step unlocks the next — do them in order.
ext.handoffSettings.telegram.step1.title: Bot token
ext.handoffSettings.telegram.step1.lead: Handoff talks to Telegram through your own bot — a small relay account you create once.
ext.handoffSettings.telegram.step1.s1: In Telegram, open @BotFather — the official bot from Telegram.
ext.handoffSettings.telegram.step1.s2: Send /newbot, then follow the prompts: choose a display name and a username ending in bot.
ext.handoffSettings.telegram.step1.s3: BotFather sends a long token (digits, colon, letters). Copy the entire line.
ext.handoffSettings.telegram.step1.s4: Paste it below and press Save token. It stays on this PC only.
ext.handoffSettings.telegram.step1.saved: To replace the bot, paste a new token from @BotFather below.
ext.handoffSettings.telegram.step2.title: Who may use the bot
ext.handoffSettings.telegram.step2.lead: A whitelist of Telegram accounts. Everyone else is ignored.
ext.handoffSettings.telegram.step2.s1: In Telegram, message @userinfobot — it replies with your numeric user ID.
ext.handoffSettings.telegram.step2.s2: Paste that number below. Several people? Separate IDs with commas, then Save.
ext.handoffSettings.telegram.step3.title: Telegram group
ext.handoffSettings.telegram.step3.lead: Cursor chats will appear as separate topics inside one group.
ext.handoffSettings.telegram.step3.s1: Create a new Telegram group and add your bot as a member.
ext.handoffSettings.telegram.step3.s2: Group menu → Topics → Enable. The group becomes a supergroup with forum threads.
ext.handoffSettings.telegram.step3.s3: Group menu → Administrators → promote the bot. Enable Manage topics, Delete messages, and Pin messages.
ext.handoffSettings.telegram.step4.title: Link this PC to the group
ext.handoffSettings.telegram.step4.lead: Tells Handoff which Telegram group belongs to this computer.
ext.handoffSettings.telegram.step4.s1: Start the Handoff server (sidebar must show Running).
ext.handoffSettings.telegram.step4.s2: Copy /register … below and send it in the group — any topic works.
ext.handoffSettings.telegram.step4.s3: When it succeeds, your account appears in this step.
ext.handoffSettings.telegram.step4.regis: To register another user, send in the group:
ext.handoffSettings.telegram.step4.sendC: Send this command in the Telegram group:
ext.handoffSettings.telegram.step4.start: Start the Handoff server — the /register command will appear here.
ext.handoffSettings.telegram.step5.title: Create chat topics
ext.handoffSettings.telegram.step5.lead: Each Cursor tab gets its own topic — that is where you write to the agent from your phone.
ext.handoffSettings.telegram.step5.s1: In the group, open the # General topic (main channel, not a project thread).
ext.handoffSettings.telegram.step5.s2: Send /bridge — Handoff creates topics for Cursor tabs that already have chat messages.
ext.handoffSettings.telegram.step5.s3: Want a topic for every open tab? Send /bridge_all instead.
ext.handoffSettings.transport.title: Bot API transport
ext.handoffSettings.transport.descriptio: Raw is the default (direct long-poll). Switch to Grammy only if you deliberately need it.
ext.handoffSettings.transport.grammy.hin: Grammy library with its handler stack. Use only if you deliberately need it.
ext.handoffSettings.transport.raw.title: Raw (recommended)
ext.handoffSettings.transport.raw.hint: Direct fetch long-poll — default for the bundled server. Switch here if Grammy hangs on startup.
ext.handoffSettings.wake.title: CursorWake
ext.handoffSettings.wake.lead: Windows tray companion — optional, for Telegram when Cursor is closed.
ext.handoffSettings.wake.s1: Listens to Telegram while Cursor is closed.
ext.handoffSettings.wake.s2: Queues messages and can launch Cursor when something arrives.
ext.handoffSettings.wake.s3: Startup keeps Wake in the tray after Windows login.
ext.handoffSettings.wake.description: Windows tray app — holds Telegram messages and can launch Cursor while it is closed.
ext.handoffSettings.wake.sidebarHint: Wake status appears in the sidebar; install and autostart live here.
ext.handoffSettings.cloud.title: Cloudflare quick tunnel
ext.handoffSettings.cloud.lead: Public HTTPS link without opening your router or installing VPN on the phone.
ext.handoffSettings.cloud.s2: Traffic is forwarded to Handoff on this PC. Web password required.
ext.handoffSettings.cloud.s3: Use /web_url in Telegram # General to post the current link.
ext.handoffSettings.cloud.description: Temporary HTTPS link (*.trycloudflare.com) so your phone needs no VPN. Requires a web password.
ext.handoffSettings.footer.settingsHint: Every setting also lives in Cursor Settings (Ctrl+, or Cmd+, — search “cursorHandoff”)
ext.handoffSettings.msg.filterCopied: Filter copied — paste into Settings search.
ext.handoffSettings.msg.customAddressReq: Enter a custom bind address first.
ext.handoffSettings.msg.allowedUsersUpda: Allowed user IDs saved. Restart the server.
ext.handoffSettings.msg.allowedUsersClea: Allowed user IDs cleared. Restart the server.
ext.handoffSettings.msg.tunnelStartFaile: Could not start tunnel — see Handoff logs.
ext.handoffSettings.msg.uninstallCloudCo: Remove cloudflared? Quick tunnel will stop.
ext.handoffSettings.diagnostics.cdp.hint: Check that Cursor answers on the debug port before starting the server.
ext.activate.passwordGenerated: CursorHandoff created a web login password: {password}
ext.activate.cloudflaredInstalledHandoff: cloudflared installed — see Handoff settings → Cloudflare
ext.server.alreadyOwner: Server is already running (this window is the owner).
ext.server.compatVersionMismatch: Handoff server bundle compatVersion mismatch ({detail}). Rebuild or install a matching VSIX.
ext.cursorUpgrade.message: Cursor {cursorVersion} — this Handoff build targets Cursor {testedCursorVersion}. Full functionality is not guaranteed. Rebuild or install the current VSIX.
ext.server.staleBundle: CursorHandoff: server started with STALE bundle — see Output / server.log
ext.server.staleKeyboards: Detected OLD code (auto-keyboards on connect) — rebuild bundle + Reload Window
ext.output.openedHint: CursorHandoff: Output panel opened. Select the «CursorHandoff» channel if empty.
ext.output.mergedMissing: CursorHandoff: merged log not ready yet — start the server; visor writes handoff.log under the runtime data folder every few seconds.
ext.cloud.uninstallFailed: Could not remove cloudflared via winget. Stop the tunnel manually or: winget uninstall Cloudflare.cloudflared
ext.sidebar.access.lanHint: 0.0.0.0 — use your LAN IP on phone
ext.sidebar.testTelegramNoToken: Save a bot token in Handoff settings first.
ext.statusBar.tooltip.disconnected: Server running, CDP not connected — click for panel
web.onboarding.tabs.title: Tabs
web.onboarding.tabs.body: Chat strip at the top — tap to switch. “all chats” lists everything; “+” opens a new Cursor chat.
web.onboarding.send.title: Send
web.onboarding.send.body: Type below and send. The message lands in the active Cursor chat on your PC.
web.onboarding.approve.title: Approve
web.onboarding.approve.body: When the agent needs permission, approve or reject in the feed.
web.onboarding.settings.title: Settings ⚙
web.onboarding.settings.body: Theme, compact feed, quick phrases, sound, and diagnostics — in the gear icon in the header.
web.cursorUpgrade.banner: Cursor {cursorVersion} — this Handoff build targets Cursor {testedCursorVersion}. Full functionality is not guaranteed. Rebuild or install the current VSIX.

web.connection.offline.emptyHint: Check Wi‑Fi, server address, or that the CursorHandoff server is running.

web.connection.relay.emptyHint: Check access to the CursorHandoff server.
web.connection.cursor.label: Waiting for Cursor
web.connection.stale.background.label: Cursor in background
web.connection.stale.background.emptyPri: Cursor connected but throttled in background.
web.connection.stale.background.emptyHin: Bring Cursor to the foreground (macOS) and wait for a snapshot.
web.connection.stale.hung.label: Cursor hung

web.connection.connected.emptyPrimary: No messages in this chat yet.
web.connection.connected.emptyHint: Send a message below or switch tab/window in Cursor.
web.connection.initial.emptyHint: Checking CursorHandoff server and Cursor IDE.

web.settings.quickPhrases.label: Up to 6 lines — buttons above the input (tap inserts text)
web.tunnel.waitHint: Link will appear in Telegram — command /web_url
web.questionnaire.title: Questions
web.questionnaire.freeformHint: Type your answer above — not in the message box below.
